calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Fiol, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Personal Representative.

The court begins by advising Mr. Fiol that Mr. Blau's fees should not have been paid without an order from the court. Counsel reports that he didn't believe that paying Mr. Blau's fees was out of the ordinary. Counsel also advises that he is willing to waive the request for extraordinary fees. The court advises counsel that payment of attorney fees in Probate matters are statutory and requires an order from the court prior to disbursement. The court then points counsel to the issue with the bond. Counsel provides oral testimony regarding the bond issue and advises that a request to waive bond was included in the initial petition. The court reminds counsel that the court's main objective is to comply with its super-fiduciary duties.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Claim Of Personal Representative And For Determination Of Priority
Off Calendar Feb-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ryan Kent for Brent & Fiol LLP
Laurence Blau for and with Administrator David Fiol

Mr. Kent stated the estate received $120,000.00 from Yellow Cab, and provided a letter from Dept. of Health Care Services dated 1/24/2020 stating the lien claim is $0.00 to the court. The Court noted it's decision will be capped on what's on the creditor's claim. Mr. Kent stated paragraph 4 was not initialed, paragraph 7 was initialed, and it doesn't change the determination that there would be an equitable lien based on the agreement. The Court noted that Welfare and Institutions Code 14124.74 supports the claim and claim for priority for no more than $167,000.00.

Petition for Approval of Claim of Personal Representative and for Determination of Priority is taken under submission.

Judge: Joeseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Claim Of Personal Representative And For Determination Of Priority
Off Calendar Jan-22-2020 Continued To Feb-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Claim Of Personal Representative And For Determination Of Priority
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Continued To Jan-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laurence Blau for and with David Fiol (Petitioner).
Milla Lvovich specially appearing for Vincent Oliverio (Beneficiary).

The Court noted that Vincent had indicated that he was going to object or file a competing petition. Ms. Lvovich stated that Vincent has agreed not to object and that he will continue on as a Beneficiary. Mr. Fiol stated that he found a safe deposit box that was abandoned and that he does not know what was in it. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required at this time. If assets are discovered, Mr. Fiol will need to file a petition for bond. Status on close of estate is set for December 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court reminds the Parties of the requirement to file an inventory and appraisal within four months.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-13-2019 Continued To Sep-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Laurence Alan Blau for David Fiol (Proposed Administrator). Terry Szucsko for Vincent Oliverio (Brother/Objector).

Mr. Szucsko stated that no written objections had been filed yet, that he will be filing a petition on behalf of his client, and that he was requesting a thirty to forty-five day continuance. Mr. Blau objected to that far of a continuance. The Court continued the Petition for Letters of Administration to September 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that it would see where things are on that date and that any filings should be on file well in advance of that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
